title: pedicularis palustris subsp. opsiantha
intro: Pedicularis palustris subsp. opsiantha, also known as marsh lousewort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bumblebee balm family (Pedicularis). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pedicularis palustris subsp. opsiantha are thought to include reducing inflammation, soothing respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. It is often prepared in herbal tea, tincture, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
